title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots may pursue Colts wideout Alec Pierce after Indianapolis opts not to franchise tag him.
Pierce, 25, led NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025, could become top free-agent receiver.
Patriots might need to restructure contracts to afford Pierce, potentially cutting Stefon Diggs for cap space.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, 34, was cited for allegedly discarding mail meant for delivery in North Troy, Vermont.
Police found opened and unopened packages and holiday cards, including one with money, in a dumpster and her vehicle.
Morisseau faces nine counts of petty larceny and five counts of unlawful mischief; court date set for March 17.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An elderly man and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough, early Tuesday morning.
The fire was reported just before 3 a.m. and consumed the single-family home rapidly, causing major damage.
Foxborough authorities, alongside neighboring fire departments, extinguished the fire within an hour; the cause remains under investigation.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston's Estella’s faces potential penalties for hosting Patriots players with strippers, hookahs, and after-hours drinking.
Owner Helder George Brandao claims Patriots players brought their own alcohol and created the party chaos at Estella's.
Boston Licensing Board to decide on possible penalties for Estella's after a police report detailed the illicit party activity.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students top the nation with the highest AP exam scores, achieving a record-breaking 35.8% scoring 3 or higher.
The state led in AP exam participation among Black or African American students, ranking third in overall graduate exam participation.
The Healey administration aims to expand Early College programs, subsidizing 90,783 AP exams for nearly 50,000 low-income students.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy City Councilors must accept a 50% pay raise, increasing their salary from $29,128.65 to $44,500.
Newly elected councilors in Quincy plan to donate part or all of their raises to nonprofits, citing trust concerns.
Discussion is ongoing about potentially repealing the raises, which the previous council approved without public hearings.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox remain a potential trade partner for Astros' Isaac Paredes despite acquiring Caleb Durbin.
Jarren Duran could be a trade piece, freeing DH spots for Masataka Yoshida and injured Triston Casas.
Trade for Paredes would create more infield competition, blocking prospects like Marcelo Mayer from regular reps.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's 2026 NFL mock draft predicts Patriots will select Texas A&M's Cashius Howell with the 31st overall pick.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks in 2025, ranking seventh among all FBS defensive linemen.
Patriots focus on enhancing their pass rush after netting only 35 sacks last season, among the league's lowest.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, was convicted of first-degree murder for the 1986 killing of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Gravel's body was discovered on Route 128 in Beverly after she was strangled, with Carey's DNA found on her clothing.
Carey, already imprisoned for attempted murder, faces life without parole for Gravel's murder; sentencing is set for March 26.

title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Diane Durgin, 67, of Weare, N.H., is accused of shooting at a Black man after he mistakenly drove to her property.
Durgin faces up to $5,000 in fines per violation of the New Hampshire Civil Rights Act for alleged racial discrimination.
Prosecutors requested an injunction barring Durgin from contacting the victim after she allegedly fired shots at his car.
